candidate_summary: AMPHORA-KV: cryptographic-hash-sealed KV cache + per-entry precision-compensation buffer for cross-session sharing.
rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7

rank_6_2603.10987.rationale: Direct match: hash-sealed KV cache with tamper detection.

rank_8_2604.02115.rationale: Direct combined match: cryptographic integrity + cross-session KV sharing.

rank_7_2510.18345.rationale: Per-entry compensation buffer for quantization precision loss.

rank_2_ceph_lmcache.rationale: Content-addressable KV (S3) is the seal-by-hash mechanism.

verifier_scores.note: Content-addressable S3-native KV connector matches seal-by-hash mechanism.
External KV cache; no seal or precision compensation.
SSD persistence only; semantic threshold-edge hit.
Multi-tier dynamic KV storage; not sealed.
Direct match: cryptographically-sealed KV cache sharing.
Quantization-aware KV with per-entry compensation buffer — the precision-compensation component.
Integrity-verified long-term KV with cross-session sharing — combined-mechanism direct match.

novelty_verdict.rationale: Three independent prior works cover the full mechanism: 2603.10987 supplies hash-seal + tamper detection; 2604.02115 supplies integrity verification + cross-session sharing (essentially the combined AMPHORA-KV claim); 2510.18345 supplies per-entry quantization compensation buffer. The candidate's only differentiator (seal + compensation in one system) is a trivial union of disclosed components, with the Ceph/LMCache stack already providing content-addressed KV sharing in production. Functional collisions across 4 distinct clusters confirm non-novelty.

decision: REJECT (non-novel; 6/8 hits, 4 functional ≥0.7 across 4 distinct effect clusters)
